clusters.title: Trump says U.S. will continue blockade after Iran says strait "completely open"
clusters.neutral_headline: Oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait 'open'
bullet_summary: Prices dropped after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" for the remainder of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire
Iran and President Trump announced that the Strait of Hormuz will now be open, but questions remain about the flow of traffic in the key water passage
French President Emmanuel Macron said he welcomed the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the Lebanon ceasefire
stock indexes hit all-time highs after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz was open for commercial vessels

items.title: Trump says U.S. will continue blockade after Iran says strait "completely open"
items.standfirst: President Trump has welcomed Iran's announcement that, at least while the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire holds, the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" to all ships.
items.content: President Trump has welcomed Iran's announcement that, at least while the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire holds, the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" to all ships.

items.title: Oil prices plunge as Iran says Strait 'open'
items.standfirst: Brent crude sinks by a tenth after Iran says the key waterway is open for commercial ships for the rest of the ceasefire.
items.content: Brent crude sinks by a tenth after Iran says the key waterway is open for commercial ships for the rest of the ceasefire.

items.title: Oil prices plummet after Iran says Strait of Hormuz is "completely open"

items.content: Prices dropped after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open" for the remainder of the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.

items.title: Iran says strait of Hormuz ‘completely open’ to commercial vessels as oil prices fall
items.standfirst: Trump claims Tehran will also suspend nuclear programme as Lebanon truce enters first full dayIran’s foreign minister has said that the strait of Hormuz is now fully open to commercial vessels, reinforcing hopes for an eventual end to the war in the Middle East and sending oil prices tumbling despite analysts’ warnings that there will be no immediate widespread resumption of passage through the vital waterway.In a barrage of social media posts, Donald Trump claimed on Friday that Iran had agreed never to close the strategic waterway again, hailing “A GREAT AND BRILLIANT DAY FOR THE WORLD!” Continue reading...
items.content: Trump claims Tehran will also suspend nuclear programme as Lebanon truce enters first full dayIran’s foreign minister has said that the strait of Hormuz is now fully open to commercial vessels, reinforcing hopes for an eventual end to the war in the Middle East and sending oil prices tumbling despite analysts’ warnings that there will be no immediate widespread resumption of passage through the vital waterway.In a barrage of social media posts, Donald Trump claimed on Friday that Iran had agreed never to close the strategic waterway again, hailing “A GREAT AND BRILLIANT DAY FOR THE WORLD!” Continue reading...

items.title: Key details on Trump's latest Strait of Hormuz announcement
items.standfirst: Iran and President Trump announced that the Strait of Hormuz will now be open, but questions remain about the flow of traffic in the key water passage. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports.
items.content: Iran and President Trump announced that the Strait of Hormuz will now be open, but questions remain about the flow of traffic in the key water passage. CBS News' Olivia Rinaldi reports.

items.content: President Trump posted on social media claims that the Strait of Hormuz will be "completely open." CBS News' Natalie Brand reports.

items.title: Macron demands assurances after Iran opens Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: French President Emmanuel Macron said he welcomed the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the Lebanon ceasefire.
items.content: French President Emmanuel Macron said he welcomed the reopening of the Strait of Hormuz and the Lebanon ceasefire.

items.title: Macron, Meloni, Starmer and Merz welcome Hormuz Strait reopening
items.standfirst: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday hosted a video conference with their European allies to discuss the possible deployment of a multinational force to the Strait of Hormuz once the war in Iran is over. Macron welcomed Iran's announcement that the waterway would be reopened to commercial shipping for the rest of the ceasefire, but called for the strait's "unconditional" reopening.
items.content: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday hosted a video conference with their European allies to discuss the possible deployment of a multinational force to the Strait of Hormuz once the war in Iran is over. Macron welcomed Iran's announcement that the waterway would be reopened to commercial shipping for the rest of the ceasefire, but called for the strait's "unconditional" reopening.

items.title: Latest news after Iran and Trump claim Strait of Hormuz is "completely open"

items.content: Iran's Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and President Trump have declared that the Strait of Hormuz is "completely open." CBS News' Charlie D'Agata and Olivia Rinaldi report.

items.title: Oil prices plunge 12% after Iran says Strait of Hormuz is open for commercial vessels
items.standfirst: Oil prices plunged and two U.S. stock indexes hit all-time highs after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz was open for commercial vessels.
items.content: Oil prices plunged and two U.S. stock indexes hit all-time highs after Iran's foreign minister said the Strait of Hormuz was open for commercial vessels.

items.title: Iran foreign minister says Strait of Hormuz ‘completely open’
items.standfirst: Iran says 'passage for all commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz' open during the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.
items.content: Iran says 'passage for all commercial vessels through Strait of Hormuz' open during the Israel-Lebanon ceasefire.

items.title: Live: Trump welcomes Iran's announcement that Strait of Hormuz is fully open to shipping
Starmer, Macron push forward efforts to reopen Strait of Hormuz
items.standfirst: French President Emmanuel Macron and UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday chair a meeting of allies to consider sending a multinational force to ensure security and free-flowing trade in the Strait of Hormuz once the current conflict between Iran and the US and Israel ends. France24 International Affairs editor Rochelle Ferguson shares further insights.
items.content: French President Emmanuel Macron and UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer on Friday chair a meeting of allies to consider sending a multinational force to ensure security and free-flowing trade in the Strait of Hormuz once the current conflict between Iran and the US and Israel ends. France24 International Affairs editor Rochelle Ferguson shares further insights.

items.title: Britain, France chair meeting on Hormuz
items.standfirst: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will on Friday host a video conference with their European allies to discuss the possible deployment of a multinational force to the Strait of Hormuz once the war in Iran is over. The force would help ensure safe and secure passage through the crucial waterway. France24 journalist Carys Garland reports from the Elysée Palace.
items.content: French President Emmanuel Macron and British Prime Minister Keir Starmer will on Friday host a video conference with their European allies to discuss the possible deployment of a multinational force to the Strait of Hormuz once the war in Iran is over. The force would help ensure safe and secure passage through the crucial waterway. France24 journalist Carys Garland reports from the Elysée Palace.

items.title: The U.S. blockade continues despite Iran's announcement the Strait of Hormuz is open
items.standfirst: Iran's foreign minister declared the Strait of Hormuz is open, following the start of an Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. President Trump swiftly responded that the U.S. naval blockade on Iran will continue.
items.content: Iran's foreign minister declared the Strait of Hormuz is open, following the start of an Israel-Lebanon ceasefire. President Trump swiftly responded that the U.S. naval blockade on Iran will continue.(Image credit: Ibrahim Amro)

items.content: The House and Senate have now passed a 10-day extension for the controversial warrantless surveillance law.

items.title: Controversial surveillance program extended by House but only until April 30
items.standfirst: The House OK'd a short-term renewal of a controversial surveillance program used by U.S. spy agencies, after Republicans revolted and refused President Trump's push for a longer extension.
items.content: The House OK'd a short-term renewal of a controversial surveillance program used by U.S. spy agencies, after Republicans revolted and refused President Trump's push for a longer extension.

items.title: Cursor In Talks to Raise $2 Billion at Over $50 Billion Value
items.standfirst: Cursor, a leading artificial intelligence startup for coding, is in advanced talks with investors to raise about $2 billion in a funding round at a valuation of more than $50 billion, not including the investment, according to a person familiar with the matter.
items.content: Cursor, a leading artificial intelligence startup for coding, is in advanced talks with investors to raise about $2 billion in a funding round at a valuation of more than $50 billion, not including the investment, according to a person familiar with the matter.

items.title: Palantir, Thales Among Companies Competing on FAA AI Tool
items.standfirst: The US Federal Aviation Administration has brought in Palantir Technologies Inc., Thales SA and Air Space Intelligence Inc. to compete on a new artificial intelligence tool for air traffic management, according to a person familiar with the matter.
items.content: The US Federal Aviation Administration has brought in Palantir Technologies Inc., Thales SA and Air Space Intelligence Inc. to compete on a new artificial intelligence tool for air traffic management, according to a person familiar with the matter.

items.title: Anthropic's Mythos Navigates a Tightrope With Washington
items.standfirst: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei is set to meet with White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles on Friday, according to reports. The meeting comes amid concerns its artificial intelligence model, Mythos, could increase cybersecurity risks. Bloomberg’s Mike Shepard breaks joins Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on “Bloomberg Tech.” (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: Anthropic CEO Dario Amodei is set to meet with White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles on Friday, according to reports. The meeting comes amid concerns its artificial intelligence model, Mythos, could increase cybersecurity risks. items.content: Dario Amodei due to speak with Susie Wiles despite lawsuits over whether AI lab is a national security threat

items.title: Anthropic’s Amodei to Meet Wiles With US Seeking Mythos Access
items.standfirst: Anthropic PBC Chief Executive Officer Dario Amodei is scheduled to meet on Friday with White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles, according to people familiar with the matter, as the Trump administration seeks wider US government access to the company’s powerful new Mythos artificial intelligence model.
items.content: Anthropic PBC Chief Executive Officer Dario Amodei is scheduled to meet on Friday with White House Chief of Staff Susie Wiles, according to people familiar with the matter, as the Trump administration seeks wider US government access to the company’s powerful new Mythos artificial intelligence model.

clusters.title: Starmer refuses to quit after ‘unforgivable’ Foreign Office vetting fiasco
clusters.neutral_headline: Is Mandelson vetting scandal the final straw for Starmer? – The Latest
bullet_summary: PM shifts blame after Peter Mandelson revealed to have failed security officials’ checks
Reported by Financial Times, Guardian World

items.title: Starmer refuses to quit after ‘unforgivable’ Foreign Office vetting fiasco
items.standfirst: PM shifts blame after Peter Mandelson revealed to have failed security officials’ checks
items.content: PM shifts blame after Peter Mandelson revealed to have failed security officials’ checks

items.title: Starmer was left in dark about Mandelson’s vetting by two other top civil servants
items.standfirst: Exclusive: Officials have spent weeks debating whether or not to release highly sensitive information about the affairKeir Starmer was left in the dark about sensitive information relating to Peter Mandelson’s security vetting by two other top civil servants, including the head of the civil service, the Guardian can reveal.The prime minister said on Friday that it was “unforgivable” and “staggering” that senior officials did not tell him that Mandelson failed a security vetting process weeks before he took up his role as ambassador to Washington. Continue reading...
items.content: Exclusive: Officials have spent weeks debating whether or not to release highly sensitive information about the affairKeir Starmer was left in the dark about sensitive information relating to Peter Mandelson’s security vetting by two other top civil servants, including the head of the civil service, the Guardian can reveal.The prime minister said on Friday that it was “unforgivable” and “staggering” that senior officials did not tell him that Mandelson failed a security vetting process weeks before he took up his role as ambassador to Washington. Continue reading...

items.title: Is Mandelson vetting scandal the final straw for Starmer? – The Latest
items.standfirst: Keir Starmer says it is ‘staggering’ and ‘unforgivable’ that he was not told Peter Mandelson had failed security vetting before taking up the role of US ambassador. The comments follow a Guardian investigation that exclusively revealed Mandelson had initially been denied clearance after a background check by security officials, but that the decision was overruled by the Foreign Office. Lucy Hough speaks to the Guardian’s head of investigations, Paul Lewis – watch on YouTube Continue reading...
items.content: Keir Starmer says it is ‘staggering’ and ‘unforgivable’ that he was not told Peter Mandelson had failed security vetting before taking up the role of US ambassador. The comments follow a Guardian investigation that exclusively revealed Mandelson had initially been denied clearance after a background check by security officials, but that the decision was overruled by the Foreign Office. Lucy Hough speaks to the Guardian’s head of investigations, Paul Lewis – watch on YouTube Continue reading...

clusters.title: Netflix Shares Tank on Weak Forecast; Hastings Steps Down
clusters.neutral_headline: Netflix Shares Tank on Weak Forecast; Hastings Steps Down
bullet_summary: In a 14-page letter to investors released on Thursday, Netflix said Hastings would not stand for re-election at its annual meeting in June and planned to focus on philanthropy and other pursuits
The streaming pioneer also announced that Chairman and co-founder Reed Hastings is stepping down from the board after 29 years to pursue philanthropy and personal interests

items.title: Netflix Shares Tank on Weak Forecast; Hastings Steps Down
items.standfirst: Netflix shares tumbled the most in four years after the company gave a forecast for the second quarter that fell short of analysts’ expectations. Bloomberg's Lucas Shaw and Wedbush Securities Senior VP Alicia Reese discuss the "overhang" of the aborted Warner Bros. Discovery deal and more with Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on “Bloomberg Tech.” (Source: Bloomberg)
items.content: Netflix shares tumbled the most in four years after the company gave a forecast for the second quarter that fell short of analysts’ expectations. Bloomberg's Lucas Shaw and Wedbush Securities Senior VP Alicia Reese discuss the "overhang" of the aborted Warner Bros. Discovery deal and more with Caroline Hyde and Ed Ludlow on “Bloomberg Tech.” (Source: Bloomberg)

items.title: Netflix co-founder Reed Hastings to leave streaming service
items.standfirst: Chair’s decision to not seek re-election ‘not as a result of any disagreement’, company says in filingReed Hastings, the Netflix chair, is leaving the streaming service he co-founded almost 30 years ago as the company regains its footing after losing out on a $72bn (£53bn) deal for Warner Bros Discovery.In a 14-page letter to investors released on Thursday, Netflix said Hastings would not stand for re-election at its annual meeting in June and planned to focus on philanthropy and other pursuits. Continue reading...
items.content: Chair’s decision to not seek re-election ‘not as a result of any disagreement’, company says in filingReed Hastings, the Netflix chair, is leaving the streaming service he co-founded almost 30 years ago as the company regains its footing after losing out on a $72bn (£53bn) deal for Warner Bros Discovery.In a 14-page letter to investors released on Thursday, Netflix said Hastings would not stand for re-election at its annual meeting in June and planned to focus on philanthropy and other pursuits. Continue reading...

items.title: Netflix Shares Fall as Guidance Disappoints Investors
items.standfirst: Netflix Inc. gave a forecast for the second quarter that fell short of analysts’ expectations, sending the shares tumbling in extended trading. The results are the first since Netflix walked away from a contentious battle for control of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. in February. The streaming pioneer also announced that Chairman and co-founder Reed Hastings is stepping down from the board after 29 years to pursue philanthropy and personal interests. Bloomberg’s Neil Campling reports.
items.content: Netflix Inc. gave a forecast for the second quarter that fell short of analysts’ expectations, sending the shares tumbling in extended trading. The results are the first since Netflix walked away from a contentious battle for control of Warner Bros. Discovery Inc. in February. The streaming pioneer also announced that Chairman and co-founder Reed Hastings is stepping down from the board after 29 years to pursue philanthropy and personal interests. Bloomberg’s Neil Campling reports.

items.title: Netflix Shares Tank on Weak Forecast; Hastings Steps Down
items.standfirst: Netflix Inc. shares tumbled the most in four years after the company gave a forecast for the second quarter that fell short of analysts’ expectations, underwhelming Wall Street just months after it lost out on a bid for Warner Bros. Discovery Inc.
items.content: Netflix Inc. shares tumbled the most in four years after the company gave a forecast for the second quarter that fell short of analysts’ expectations, underwhelming Wall Street just months after it lost out on a bid for Warner Bros. Discovery Inc.
